content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented: create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment, registers builtin filters then env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None (so passed-in filters override builtins). prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) now supports @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); internally calls Prompt._template_from_str(docstring, filters). Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(path, filters=None) both accept filters and pass to _template_from_str/_template_from_file, which now just call create_jinja_env(loader, filters). All existing tests pass (13/13). Go ahead and wire your global registry into create_jinja_env - e.g. merge global registry dict first, then apply the local  arg on top so local overrides global, keeping current override order (any override of a builtin name) intact.

fields.note: Implemented: create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment, registers builtin filters then env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None (so passed-in filters override builtins). prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) now supports @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); internally calls Prompt._template_from_str(docstring, filters). Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(path, filters=None) both accept filters and pass to _template_from_str/_template_from_file, which now just call create_jinja_env(loader, filters). All existing tests pass (13/13). Go ahead and wire your global registry into create_jinja_env - e.g. merge global registry dict first, then apply the local  arg on top so local overrides global, keeping current override order (any override of a builtin name) intact.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Concern: our patches are each graded standalone, the evaluator only reads my own patch.txt applied to a pristine repo, so I cannot reference GLOBAL_FILTERS in my copy since that name does not exist there - it would break my submission with a NameError. Instead: create_jinja_env(loader, filters) already accepts a plain dict and does filters-override-builtins via env.filters.update(filters). That dict parameter is the integration point. In your own patch, when you add register_filter/unregister_filter plus your global registry, also add your own merge step inside your own copy of create_jinja_env or the template methods, so your patch stays self-contained too. Both patches stay independently correct even though the diffs touch similar lines. Sound OK?
